1

Imfuyo Farm: Operational and HR System Profile
1 Overview
Imfuyo Farm is a Kenya-based agritech farm operating mixed livestock and crop systems

. It
uses structured field data capture, basic analytics pipelines, and KPI-driven farm manage-
ment.
Primary objective: maximize output per unit input (feed, land, labor).
2 Operations
2.1 Livestock Unit
Scale: small-medium poultry and dairy units.
Daily structure:
• Feed allocation logged per batch (kg/day per flock or herd unit)
• Mortality recorded within 24 hours of detection
• Weight sampling every 7 days (growth tracking)
• Vaccination cycles scheduled per production batch (e.g., 21–42 day poultry cycles)
Key metric system:
• FCR (Feed Conversion Ratio) used for efficiency benchmarking
• Mortality rate tracked weekly (target: minimized variance per cycle)
2.2 Crop Unit

enchmarking
• Mortality rate tracked weekly (target: minimized variance per cycle)
2.2 Crop Unit
Cycle-based production aligned with seasonal rainfall.
Operational rules:
• Soil test required before planting cycle initiation
• Fertilizer application split into 2–3 controlled stages
• Irrigation adjusted based on rainfall deviation logs
Performance metrics:
• Yield per acre benchmarked against prior cycle baseline
1

• Input-to-output ratio (seed + fertilizer vs harvest yield)
2.3 Data and Technology Layer
Lightweight agritech stack:
• Mobile forms (field staff input daily logs)
• Spreadsheet or database aggregation layer
• Simple dashboards for KPI visualization
• Python scripts for cleaning and aggregation
Data latency: daily sync cycle.
3 Key KPIs
Operational decisions depend on:
• Cost per unit (KES per kg or animal)
• Yield per acre (kg/acre or tons/acre)
• Mortality rate (%)
• FCR (feed efficiency ratio)
• Revenue per cycle (crop or livestock batch)
4 HR System
4.1 Workforce Composition
• Field operations staff (feeding, planting, maintenance)
• Data/tech support (systems, dashboards, analytics)
• Supervisors (unit-level coordination)
• Interns (project-based contributors)
4.2 Onboarding (14-Day Cycle)
Day 1–3:
• Farm layout orientation
• Safety induction (equipment + livestock handling)
• System introduction (data capture tools)
Day 4–7:
• Shadow assigned unit supervisor
• First supervised data entry task
2

• Workflow familiarization
Day 8–14:
• Independent task execution
• First measurable output delivered
• Performance baseline assessment
4.3 Working Structure
• Standard hours: 08:00–17:00 (Mon–Fri)
• Livestock unit starts: as early as 06:00
• Tech roles: partial flexibility (up to 40% remote where applicable)
4.4 Compensation Model
• Intern stipend: KES 5,000–15,000/month
• Staff: benchmarked against Kenyan agri-sector medians
• Performance bonus: tied to measurable KPI improvements (yield, efficiency, cost reduction)
4.5 Leave Policy
• Annual leave: 21 working days
• Sick leave: up to 7 days per cycle
• Emergency leave: approved within 24 hours based on severity
4.6 Performance Evaluation
Evaluation frequency:
• Interns: monthly
• Staff: quarterly
Scoring dimensions:
• Task completion accuracy
• Data reliability (error rate in logs)
• Initiative in process improvement
• Contribution to KPI movement
4.7 Internship Design
Duration: 8–12 weeks.
Structure:
3

• Assigned supervisor per intern
• Minimum 1 measurable project
• Final deliverable required (dashboard, dataset, automation, or analysis report)
Typical projects:
• Livestock tracking system dashboard
• Farm data pipeline automation
• Yield trend analysis per season
5 Interview-Relevant Logic
Candidates are expected to demonstrate:
• Translation of raw data into operational decisions
• Cost-efficiency reasoning under constraints
• Ability to design small but functional systems
• Practical thinking in farm or field environments
6 Conclusion
Imfuyo Farm operates as a KPI-driven agritech system combining field operations and lightweight
data infrastructure

. The emphasis is practical efficiency, measurable improvement, and re-
peatable farm system design.
4
